name: Sourest Sourdough
recipeIngredient: Gaye Levy DTXT63A 1 tbsp Olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Set the sourdough starter out on a counter and bring it to room temperature. Feed as usual, allowing it to become foamy and active. This takes about 8–12 hours.
2. Combine all ingredients in the bread machine and start the dough cycle.
3. When the cycle is complete, remove the dough and gently squeeze out gases. Cover with a damp towel and let rest for 20–30 minutes.
4. Sprinkle cornmeal on a board and shape the dough into two cylindrical loaves.
5. Place the loaves on a cornmeal-covered baking sheet or a special baguette pan. Cover again with a damp towel and refrigerate for 12–24 hours.
6. Remove dough from the refrigerator, sprinkle with water, and let rise until fully risen (they may continue to rise slightly in the refrigerator).
7. Spray the loaves lightly with water before baking at 375°F for 30 minutes or until golden brown and crusty. For a crisper crust, spray every five minutes during baking.
8. Note: Bread will vary based on temperature, humidity, and starter mood. It can be very sour and dense or mildly sour and fluffy. Using a special baguette pan helps maintain shape. Variations include using more or less sugar, substituting vinegar for water, or using flat beer instead of water when feeding the starter or baking.
PLEASE NOTE: This recipe consistently receives praise as one of the best sourdough breads.
